channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Follow NPR's annotated fact check of President Trump's address to Congress
item.description: As President Donald Trump addresses a joint session of Congress tonight, reporters from across NPR's newsroom will fact check and offer context as the speech unfolds.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s drive to boost domestic mining stokes long-running debate near BWCA
item.description: As President Donald Trump lifts “undue burdens” on U.S. mines, the shift in the federal posture could boost long-simmering projects in northern Minnesota. Meanwhile, efforts at the state level to slow mining face dim prospects.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Suspect in Kabul airport bombing during Afghanistan pullout is caught and heading to US, Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A suspected senior planner in the suicide bombing at the Kabul airport that killed 13 American service members during the chaotic U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats disrupt, protest and wear pained expressions for Trump’s speech
item.description: House Democrats greeted President Trump’s return to Congress on Tuesday with a spirited series of disruptive protests – and a number of painful looks as they suffered through an address in which the returning president repeatedly cajoled and mocked them.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to press ahead on reshaping America in speech to Congress as Democrats register dissent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump vowed Tuesday to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy in an unyielding address before Congress that left Democratic legislators to register their dissent with stone faces, p
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: 'A new president' has solved the border crisis
item.description: The president said that a change in leadership, not new legislation, was the key to controlling border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's address to Congress showed the country's stark partisan divide
item.description: A president’s address to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 13-year-old cancer survivor earns standing ovation as he becomes Secret Service agent during Trump speech
item.description: A 13-year-old cancer survivor named DJ Daniel was honored by President Trump during his speech to Congress on Tuesday night and made a Secret Service member.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington, DC, to remove 'Black Lives Matter' painting from street near White House, mayor says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation's capital city will remove the large painting of the words “Black Lives Matter” on a street one block from the White House as Washington Mayor Muriel Bowser struggles to fend off threats of encroachment from both President Donald Trump and the Republican-controlled Congr
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Part of pro-Palestinian student art exhibit at UNT removed amid antisemitism complaints by state lawmakers
item.description: Five GOP lawmakers who targeted the exhibit also called for the cancellation of the “Palestinian Children and the Politics of Genocide” lecture, which is still scheduled.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge orders longer-term nationwide block on Trump orders on transgender youth health care
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge extended a nationwide block Tuesday on President Donald Trump’s executive orders halting federal funding for providers of gender-affirming health care for transgender people under age 19.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOPers hope Trump keeps door to mineral deal open for Ukraine despite Oval Office dispute
item.description: House Republicans said the proposed minerals deal between the U.S. and Ukraine should stay on the table despite the explosive Oval Office meeting Friday between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE slashes nearly $1M for alpaca farming in Peru, other questionable grants in latest wasteful spending cut
item.description: DOGE announced on Tuesday it canceled a number of grants, including those for alpaca farming in Peru and reducing social discrimination against recyclers in Bolivia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Democrats will respond to Trump's Congress address
item.description: The administration says the theme will be the renewal of the American dream and comes amid sweeping cuts to the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department is reviewing prosecution of Colorado clerk who supported Trump’s election lies
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The Department of Justice is backing a former county election clerk in Colorado who was convicted for her role in allowing supporters of President Donald Trump to access confidential data about the 2020 election, the latest move by the administration to use its power to reward allies w
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINA CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance's comments about European peacekeeping plan for Ukraine draw ire abroad
item.description: Vice President Vance said the best security guarantee for Ukraine is to sign a mineral deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans advised to avoid in-person town halls amid protests
item.description: House Republicans are being encouraged to avoid in-person town halls in favor of phone and livestreamed versions, advice that comes after constituents and activists rocked recent GOP events by expressing outrage about the Trump administration’s dramatic moves to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Linda McMahon has been confirmed as Trump’s secretary of education
item.description: The White House has been clear that it intends to dismantle the U.S. Department of Education, and that it will be McMahon’s job to oversee that effort.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny , Cory Turner, and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy wants peace, ready to accept minerals deal after WH blowup
item.description: President Donald Trump says Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy has written to him to say he appreciates U.S. support for his country in its war with Russia and is ready to sign a deal that could ensure future American support.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 2 Las Vegas teens present antisemitism bill at Nevada Legislature in Carson City
item.description: Two Las Vegas high school students helped put antisemitism in the public eye on Monday as they testified at the Nevada Legislature on one bill and helped present another.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hegseth orders suspension of Pentagon's offensive cyberoperations against Russia
item.description: A U.S. official, speaking on condition of anonymity to discuss sensitive operations, on Monday confirmed the pause.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance's role in Zelenskyy clash puts spotlight on VP
item.description: Vice President JD Vance's vocal involvement was among several noteworthy moments and led to mixed reactions.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Latex glove ban proposed in Nevada to help allergy sufferers
item.description: An allergy to latex can come with a lot of limits on where you can go and what you can do. Some clothing, medical procedures, and even visits to restaurants become potential problems.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada film tax bill would bring jobs, but questions remain as lawmakers examine details
item.description: Lawmakers dug into details of the film tax credit bill on Thursday, with film studio executives from Warner Bros. Discovery and Sony answering questions in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
